sections.name: tool-usage
sections.feedback: Break up into domain-specific tags like Tasklet instead of one monolithic <tool-usage> block. Each domain gets its own top-level XML tag. Also removes duplication with the separate <triggers> section.
changes: Remove the monolithic <tool-usage> wrapper
Create <crm> tag for CRM Reading + Writing guidance
Create <file-storage> tag for file storage guidance
Create <web> tag for web search/scrape guidance
Create <calculations> tag for calculate tool guidance
Create <pdf-documents> tag for generate_pdf guidance
Move Triggers sub-section content into the existing <triggers> tag (section 6) and deduplicate
Keep the one-liner intro ('You have tools across six categories...') or drop it — each section is self-explanatory

sections.name: custom-skills
sections.feedback: Two changes: (1) Remove rule 4 'Do NOT mention that you're using a skill' — we WANT the agent to announce which skill it's using, easier to debug. (2) Add the skills directory structure like Tasklet does.
changes: Replace 'Do NOT mention that you're using a skill' with: announce the skill at start, e.g. 'I'm using the daily-briefing skill to prepare your morning briefing.'
Add skills directory structure tree showing system/, connections/, and user-created skill paths
Follow Tasklet's structure as closely as possible

sections.name: triggers
sections.feedback: Rewrite to match Tasklet's structure almost identically. Keep Sunder-specific additions (instruction_path, subagent delegation, /agent/subagents/ path). Merge in the Triggers sub-section from <tool-usage> (section 3).
changes: Rewrite opening to explain the mental model like Tasklet: triggers fire events, you get invoked with a system message, you handle the event
Add 'gather all info upfront — you will not be able to ask the user later for clarification'
Add 'make sure all pre-requisite work is completed (connections, files, etc.) to ensure error-free execution'
Add 'make sure you have activated all needed tools from all needed connections'
Keep Sunder-specific: instruction_path handling, subagent delegation preference, /agent/subagents/{trigger-name}.md path convention
Keep Sunder-specific: 'do not test unless the user asks' (matches Tasklet)
Merge duplicate trigger guidance from <tool-usage> section into here

sections.name: subagents
sections.feedback: Rewrite to match Tasklet's structure almost identically. Keep Sunder-specific differences (connection tools not inherited, no SQL database, no 'should not be mentioned to the user').
changes: Rewrite opening to match Tasklet: 'reduce context size and costs by handling tasks in isolation'
Add 'instructions must be completely self-contained' framing
Add 'only final message returned; execution details hidden to conserve context'
Add 'subagents should report errors in their response so the parent can improve their instructions'
Add <when-to-create> sub-tag: MUST STRONGLY CONSIDER for recurring triggers, large context, repeated workflows. ALWAYS check for existing subagents first.
Add <managing-subagents> sub-tag: manage via write_file/read_file, update when users give feedback, include .md file structure template
Keep Sunder-specific: connection tools not inherited, cannot create connections/triggers/send messages/use browser
Keep Sunder-specific: external-facing actions should stay on parent run
Drop 'should not be mentioned to the user' — we want transparency for debugging (consistent with skills announcement change)
